title: Linked Worktrees and Parallel Branch Operations · Graph Lens: Gita Worktree Common Perworktree
summary: A maintainer must review a patch, repair production, compare two releases, and build documentation simultaneously without repeated checkout or duplicated object storage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita worktree common perworktree increment in multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Worktree Common Perworktree
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G65 is a theory in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Worktree Common Perworktree contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish common repository data from per-worktree head, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G65 is a worked example in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair. A defensible solution applies Gita Worktree Common Perworktree, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved
git worktree list --porcelain
# expected: porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state
# observation: the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason
# changed-case: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair
# transfer-limit: worktrees share refs and objects, so concurrent real maintenance and rewrites need coordination
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Each linked worktree contains an independent clone and object database. This fails the Gita Worktree Common Perworktree contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Worktree Common Perworktree evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-worktree-common-perworktree-1na9st" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Worktree Common Perworktree into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-worktree-common-perworktree-1na9st
# scenario-key: trace-1na9st probe-ts9an1 boundary-a9st1n owner-1na9stx fault-1na9stz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1n candidate=b00b1st
# hypothesis: a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved
git worktree list --porcelain
# expected: porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state
# observation: the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason
# changed-case: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision
# after-ref: main=a11ce1n candidate=c0ffeet
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair
# transfer-limit: worktrees share refs and objects, so concurrent real maintenance and rewrites need coordination
